title: Shabbat Without a Jewish Community: Keeping Shabbat Alone in China

sources.title: 10 Tips for Preparing for Shabbat Alone
sources.author: Rabbi Menachem Posner
key_points: Actual observance of Shabbat is personal, does not require congregation
Pre-cut paper towels and liquid soap for hand washing
Call friends/family before Shabbat for social connection
Pray at same time as local congregation

sources.title: The Shabbat Laws
key_points: Shabbat observance does not require community/congregation
Core practices can be performed individually

sources.title: Ask the Expert: Shabbat Alone
key_points: Spend Shabbat with observant family when possible
Rabbi Abraham Joshua Heschel: 'The meaning of the Sabbath is to celebrate time rather than space'

sources.title: Exodus 20:8-11 - The Shabbat Commandment
key_points: Remember the sabbath day and keep it holy
Six days labor, seventh day rest

cultural_parallels: Daoist concepts of natural cycles and rest
Historical Jewish community in Kaifeng maintained traditions in isolation
Shanghai Jewish refugees created community from scratch
